title: First-Use Auto-Detection and Global Skill

description.summary: Add automatic first-use detection so the TC skill prompts for initialization in any new project without requiring manual invocation
description.motivation: User wants TC tracking to be truly zero-touch for new projects. Without this, users must remember to run /tc init manually. A global skill ensures every Claude Code session checks for TC tracking and offers setup.
description.detailed_design: Two-part implementation: (1) Global skill file at ~/.claude/skills/tc.md that loads in every Claude Code session and checks for docs/TC/tc_config.json on startup. If missing, prompts user to run /tc init. (2) Updated SKILL.md with a 'First-Use Detection' section documenting this behavior.

files_affected.description: Added 'First-Use Detection (MANDATORY)' section between header and Overview

next_steps: Test in a fresh directory to verify auto-detection prompt
User approval to transition to tested/deployed
key_context: Global skill at ~/.claude/skills/tc.md — loads in every Claude Code session
SKILL.md updated with First-Use Detection section

decisions_made.decision: Use global skill file rather than a hook
decisions_made.rationale: Skills are the Claude Code mechanism for auto-loading behavior. A global skill at ~/.claude/skills/ loads in every session regardless of project, which is exactly the behavior needed.

notes: The global skill file (~/.claude/skills/tc.md) is installed on the user's machine but not tracked in the git repo since it's user-local configuration.
